cards.back: Data stored and processed as binary digits.
Continuous data, such as sound or light before it is converted into digital form.

cards.front: What process converts analogue sound or light into digital form?
cards.back: Analogue to digital conversion, involving recording, sampling and storing.

cards.front: Name two advantages of storing data digitally.
cards.back: Examples include easier retrieval, efficiency, security, accessibility and scalability.

cards.front: Name two disadvantages of storing data digitally.
cards.back: Examples include loss of quality due to sampling, expense and management issues.
A basic element used to store/display a digital image.
The level of detail in a digital image.

cards.front: Name two types of digital graphics.
What can computer systems store and process?
Name four storage media in the specification.
cards.back: Magnetic, optical, cloud and solid state.
